description: Following the breath and walking in silence are both 'phirena'. The verb covers deliberate meditation practice across methods and postures. 'theonu' (attend) directs attention in any setting; 'romishe' (contemplate) considers a subject at length.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close without sealing them for unvoiced 'ph'. Send air through the gap before high-front unrounded 'i'. Tap or briefly trill at the alveolar ridge, then enter stressed true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n'; lower the velum for the nasal and open into central 'a'.
usage_notes: The verb usually stands without an object. Surrounding phrases can give time, place, posture, or method. Regular practice takes habitual 'ro'. Noun 'phirena' means meditation or a meditation session. Exact names of traditions and techniques remain in source material when their identity matters.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A restless session is still 'phirena'. Calm and insight are possible results, not tests for whether the practice occurred.

semantic_domains.cognition: deliberate contemplative practice involving attention and awareness
semantic_domains.ritual: meditation practised as a repeated personal or shared observance
